description: Empidonax flycatcher with longish primaries, black tertials with white edging. Dark wing coverts with broad moderately buff wingbars. Greenish back, nape, crown, with olive gray auriculars, set off from white throat. Traill's bill. Relatively large tailed, like Traill's, but crown consistently flat, typical for Alder and not for Willow Flys. Quite narrow white eyering. Belly and vent washed with pale yellow. Chest with some dusky to sides.Behaviors: Seemed well fed. Mostly sat perched, occasionally sallying forth for insects.Call: not heardPlumage: immature
